hadiths.chapter: 89. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FOR AN HEIR
hadiths.ar:
5444   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْحَّح  َّالِ عَّن ْ ي َّعْلَّن َّه َّ ن ْن ِ مَّي ْمُّون ٍ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ ق َّي ْسٍ ق َّال سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّق َّص ِّلُّ ن َّعْص َّ وُّلْذِةِ عَّلَّى ن َّعْص ٍ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
The author of this book (may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him) said that another 
narration states "There is no will for an heir". 
[AL SADUQ] 
This does not contradict the previous H. Its meaning is that an heir cannot receive a 
bequest exceeding one -third of the estate, just as a non -heir cannot be bequeathed 
more than one-third. 
[REFERENCES] Da’a’im Al-Islam, Vol.2 p.358 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.194 • Al-Muqni’ah, Vol.1 
p.670 • Fiqh Al-Quran, Vol.2 p.300 • Mutashabih Al-Quran wa Mukhtalifah, Vol.2 p.214 • Sa’d Al-Sa’ud, Vol.1 
p.150 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.14 p.101  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 89, H. 5443
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FOR AN HEIR
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FOR AN HEIR
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٨٩، ح ٥٤٤٣

hadiths.chapter: 89. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FOR AN HEIR
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Mu hammad Al -Hajjal from Tha ʿlabah ibn 
Maymun from Muhammad ibn Qays, who said:  
I asked Abu Jafar Imam Mu hammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} about a man who gives 
preference to some of his children over others. 
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, and he may also favor some of his wives (over other wives)."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 7 p.10 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.195 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 23 p.1395 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.244 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.288

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 89, H. 5444
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FOR AN HEIR
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS FOR AN HEIR

hadiths.chapter: 90. CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5446  -  وَّ رَّوَّى رِن ْعِى    عَّن ِ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلِ ن ْن ِ ي َّسَّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ي ُّوصَّى إ ِلَّي ْهِ  ق َّال 
ن ِهَّا إ ِلَّي ْهِ مِن ْ ن َّلَّذٍ ق َّلَّي ْسَّ لَّهُّ رَّذ ُّهَّا وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق ِى  مِصْزٍ ي ُّوج َّذُّ ق ِي هِ ع َّي ْزُّةُّ ق َّذ َّإك َّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Rib ʿi ibn Abdullah from Mu hammad ibn 
Muslim from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
"If a man appoints someone as his executor while that person is absent, the appointed 
person does not have the right to reject the will. However, if he appoints him while he 
is present in the same town, then he has the choice to either accept or decline it."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.6 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.195 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.205 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.81 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.319 • Al-Fusul Al-Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.316  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 90, H. 5445
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٠، ح ٥٤٤٥

hadiths.chapter: 90. CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5447   -  وَّ رَّوَّى سَّهْلُّ ن ْن ُّ ر ِي َّاذٍ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إَّلز َّي  َّان ِ ق َّالَّ : كَّي َّن ْث ُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ل  ذَّعَّاةُّ وَّإلِذُّةُّ إ ِلَّى  
ق َّن ُّولِ وَّصِي  َّي ِهِ هَّلْ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّمْي َّن ِغَّ مِن ْ ق َّن ُّولِ وَّصِي  َّه ِ وَّإلِذِةِ ق َّوَّق  َّغَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّل س َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
Ribʿi narrated from Al -Fuḍayl ibn Yasar from Abu Abdullah I mam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} regarding a man who is appointed as an executor.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If the will is sent to him from another town, he does not have the right 
to reject it. However, if he is in a city where others are available, then it is up to him to 
accept or decline it." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.6 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.195 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.205 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.81 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.320  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 90, H. 5446
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٠، ح ٥٤٤٦

hadiths.chapter: 90. CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5448  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ هِس َّامِ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلز ج ُّلِ ي ُّوصِى  إ ِلَّى 
إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ن ِوَّصِي  َّه ٍ ق َّي َّكْزَّةُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّق ْن َّلَّهَّا ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ذ ُّلْهُّ عَّلَّى هَّذ ِةِ إَّلْحَّالِ
hadiths.en:
Sahl ibn Ziyad narrated from Ali ibn Al-Rayyan, who said: 
I wrote to Abu Al-Hasan {a.s} asking about a man whose father invited him to accept his 
will. Is it permissible for him to refuse accepting his father's will?  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He does not have the right to refuse." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.7 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.195 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.206 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.83 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.322  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 90, H. 5447
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٠، ح ٥٤٤٧

hadiths.chapter: 90. CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5449  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّكَّمِ عَّن ْ سَّي ْف ِ ن ْن ِ عَّمِي زَّة َّ عَّن ْ مَّن ْصُّورِ ن ْن ِ جَّار ِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ لس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ :  
«  كَّان َّ س َّاهِذإً ق َّا َّن َّى إ َّن ْ ي َّق ْن َّلَّهَّا طَّلَّث َّ ع َّي ْزَّة
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who is entrusted with a will by another man but dislikes 
accepting it. 
Abu Abdullah {a.s} said: "He should not abandon him in this situation." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.6 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.196 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.321  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 90, H. 5448
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST

hadiths.chapter: 90. CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Al-Hakam narrated from Sayf ibn Umayrah from Manṣur ibn Hazim from 
Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
"If a man appoints his brother as his executor while he is absent, he does not have the 
right to reject the will. This is because if he had been present and refused to accept it, 
the testator would have sought someone else." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.6 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.196 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.206 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.82 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.320

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 90, H. 5449

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REFUSING TO ACCEPT A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٠، ح ٥٤٤٩

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5451   -  وَّ رَّوَّى صَّق ْوَّإن ُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّحْي َّى عَّن ْ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ن َّكْزٍ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّ ق َّال ع ُّلاَّمِ عَّس ْزُّ 
سِن ِي ن َّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ لَّهُّ ق ِى  مَّالِهِ مَّا إ َّعْي َّق َّ إ َّوْ ي َّصَّذ َّق َّ وَّ إ َّوْصَّى عَّلَّى جَّذٍّ مَّعْزُّوف ٍ وَّ جَّق ٍّ ق َّهُّوَّ ج َّان ِز
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Aban ibn Uthman from Abd Al-
Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, 
who said:  
"When a boy reaches the age of ten, his will becomes valid." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.28 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.196 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.269 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.165 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.362  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 91, H. 5450
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٥٤٥٠

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5452  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْمَّع ْزَّإءِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس لاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال 
ن َّلَّغ َّ إَّلْع ُّلاَّمُّ عَّس ْزَّ سِن ِي ن َّ ق َّا َّوْصَّى ن ِي ُّلُّث ِ مَّالِهِ ق ِى  جَّق ٍّ ج َّار َّب ْ وَّصِي  َّي ُّهُّ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ   كَّان َّ إِن ْن َّ سَّن ْغِ سِن ِي ن َّ ق َّا َّوْصَّى مِن ْ مَّالِهِ 
ن ِالْي َّسِي زِ ق ِى  جَّق ٍّ ج َّار َّب ْ وَّصِي  َّي ُّه
hadiths.en:
Ṣafwan ibn Yahya narrated from Musa ibn Bakr from Zurarah from Abu Jafar 
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said: 
"When a boy reaches the age of ten, whatever he does with his wealth - whether he 
frees a slave, gives charity, or makes a will within reasonable limits and rightful 
matters - is valid." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.28 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.197 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.181 
• Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.239 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.270 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.166 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 
p.211 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.362  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 91, H. 5451
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٥٤٥١

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5453  - وَّ رَّوَّى عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحَّكَّمِ عَّن ْ ذَّإوُّذَّ ن ْن ِ إَّلن  ُّعْمَّان ِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إ َّي  ُّوب َّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْ ث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ 
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول   ج َّار َّب ْ وَّصِي  َّي ُّهُّ لِذ َّوِى  إَّلْأ َّرْجَّامِ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّح ُّز ْ 
لِلْع ُّزَّن َّاءِ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abi Al-Maghra from Abu Baṣir from 
Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: "If a boy reaches the 
age of ten and makes a will for one -third of his wealth in a rightful cause, his will is 
valid. And if he is seven years old and makes a will for a small amount of his wealth in 
a rightful cause, his will is also valid." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.29 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.197 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.182 
• Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.270 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.167 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.361  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 91, H. 5452
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٥٤٥٢

hadiths.chapter: 91. CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Al -Hakam narrated from Dawud ibn Al -Nuʿman from Abu Ayyub from 
Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah {a.s} say: 
"If a boy is on his deathbed and makes a will before reaching maturity, his will is valid 
if it is for his close relatives, but it is not valid for strangers."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.28 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.197 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.181 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.360

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 91, H. 5453
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON THE AGE AT WHICH A CHILD'S BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩١، ح ٥٤٥٣

hadiths.chapter: 92. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
hadiths.ar:
5455  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّجْمَّذَّ إَّلْأ َّس ْعَّزِى  ُّ عَّن ِ إَّلسِّن ْذِى ِّ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ ي ُّون ُّسَّ ن ْن ِ ي َّعْق ُّوب َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  مَّزْ ي َّمَّ ذ َّكَّزَّةُّ عَّن ْ 
إ َّن ِي هِ : إ َّن  َّ إ ُّمَّامَّه َّ ن ِن ْث َّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّاضِ وَّ إ ُّم ُّهَّا ر َّي ْن َّث ُّ ن ِن ْث ُّ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ  عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ كَّان َّث ْ ي َّحْث َّ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  طَّالِث ٍ  
عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ن َّعْذَّ ق َّاطِمَّه َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّح َّلَّف َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا ن َّعْذَّ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّ ة ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلن  َّوْق َّلِ ق َّذ َّكَّزَّ إ َّن  َّهَّا وَّج ِعَّث ْ  
وَّج َّعاً س َّذِي ذإً جَّي  َّ ى إُّعْي ُّق ِلَّ لِسَّان ُّهَّا ق َّح َّاءَّهَّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ وَّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ إِن ْن َّا عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ هِى َّ لأَّ ي َّسْي َّطِي غُّ  إَّلْكَّلاَّمَّ 
ق َّح َّعَّلاَّ ي َّق ُّولأَّن ِ لَّهَّا وَّ إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ُّ كَّارِة  لِذ َّلِك إ ْسِهَّا ن َّعَّمْ 
ي ُّس ِي زُّ ن ِزَّإ ْسِهَّا إ َّن ْ ن َّعَّمْ لأَّ ي ُّق ْصِحُّ ن ِالْكَّلاَّمِ ق َّا َّج َّار َّ ذ َّلِك َّ لَّهَّا.
hadiths.en:
Abd Al-Ṣamad ibn Muhammad narrated from Hanan ibn Sadir from his father, 
from Abu Ja‘far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, who said: 
"I entered upon Mu hammad ibn Ali ibn Al -Hanafiyya, and his tongue had become 
paralyzed.  
I instructed him to make his will, but he did not respond. So, I ordered a basin to be 
brought and filled it with sand.  
I said to him, 'Write with your hand,' and he wrote his will in the sand with his hand, 
and I copied it onto a scroll." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.197 • Kamal Al-Din, Vol.1 p.36 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 
p.241 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.29 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.372  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 92, H. 5454
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٥٤٥٤

hadiths.chapter: 92. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
hadiths.ar:
5456  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْهَّمَّذ َّإن ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : كَّي َّن ْث ُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ل  كَّي َّث َّ كِي َّان اً ن ِح َّطِّهِ 
وَّ لَّمْ ي َّق ُّلْ لِوَّرَّي َّي ِهِ هَّذ ِةِ وَّصِي  َّي ِى  وَّ لَّمْ ي َّق ُّلْ إ ِن ِّى  ق َّذْ إ َّوْصَّي ْث ُّ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ  كَّي َّث َّ كِي َّان اً ق ِي هِ مَّا إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّوصِى َّ ن ِهِ هَّلْ ي َّح ِث ُّ 
عَّلَّى وَّرَّي َّي ِهِ إَّلْق ِي َّامُّ ن ِمَّا ق ِى  إَّلْكِي َّاب ِ ن ِح َّطِّهِ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّا ْمُّزْهُّمْ ن ِذ َّلِك َّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  كَّان َّ لَّهُّ وُّلْذ  ي ُّن ْق ِذ ُّون َّ كُّل َّ س َّى ْءٍ  
ي َّح ِذُّون َّ ق ِى  كِي َّاب ِ إ َّن ِي هِمْ ق ِى  وَّج ْهِ إَّلْن ِزِّ إ َّوْ ع َّي ْزِةِ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Ahmad Al-Ashari narrated from Al-Sindi ibn Muhammad from 
Yunus ibn Yaʿqub from Abu Maryam, who narrated from his father: 
Umamah bint Abi Al-Aṣ - whose mother was Zaynab, the daughter of the Messenger of 
Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) - was married to Imam Ali ibn Abi Ṭalib 
{a.s} after Sayyida Faṭimah {s.a} (passed away).  
After the martyrdom of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, Al-Mughirah ibn Al -Nawfal married 
her. 
It was narrated that she became severely ill until her tongue became paralyzed. Imam 
Hasan ibn Ali {a.s} and Imam Hussain ibn Ali {a.s}, the sons of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, 
came to her while she was unable to speak.  
They {a.s} began asking her, while Al-Mughirah disapproved.  
They {a.s} asked: "Did you emancipate so-and-so and his family?" She nodded her head 
in agreement. 
They {a.s} continued: "And this and that?"  
She again nodded her head in affirmation but could not speak clearly.  
Her gestures were accepted as valid in confirming her wishes.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.198 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.9 p.241 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 
p.29 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.373 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.22 p.157  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 92, H. 5455
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٢، ح ٥٤٥٥

hadiths.chapter: 92. CHAPTER ON BEQUESTS MADE THROUGH WRITING
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Ibrahim ibn Muhammad Al-Hamadhani, who said: 
I wrote to Abu Al -Hasan Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} regarding a man who wrote 
something in his own handwriting but did not tell his heirs, "This is my will," nor did he 
say, "I have made a will."  However, he wrote a document that contained what he 
intended to bequeath. 
I asked whether it was obligatory for his heirs to fulfill what was written in his 
handwriting, even though he did not command them to do so.  
Abu Al-Hasan {a.s} replied: "If he had children, they must carry out everything they find 
written in their father's document, whether it pertains to acts of righteousness or 
anything else." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.198 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.269 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.30 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.372

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 92, H. 5456

hadiths.chapter: 93. CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5458   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ ن ُّكَّي ْزِ ن ْن ِ إ َّعْي َّن َّ عَّن ْ عُّن َّي ْذِ ن ْن ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إ لل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
ي َّق ُّول
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Faḍḍal narrated from Ali ibn Uqbah from Burayd Al -ʿIjli 
from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
"The one who makes a will has the right to revoke it or make changes to it as long as he 
is alive." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.12 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.199 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.190 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.73 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.303  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 93, H. 5457
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٣، ح ٥٤٥٧

hadiths.chapter: 93. CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5459 - وَّ رَّوَّى ي ُّون ُّسُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْكَّان َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  ق َّال 
إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّ إَّلْمُّذَّن  َّزَّ مِن َّ إَّلي  ُّلُّث ِ وَّ إ َّن  َّ لِلز َّج ُّلِ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْق ُّ ص َّ وَّصِي  َّي َّهُّ ق َّي َّز ِي ذَّ ق ِي هَّا وَّ ي َّن ْق ُّصَّ مِن ْهَّا مَّا لَّمْ ي َّمُّث ْ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Bukayr ibn A ʿyan from Ubayd ibn 
Zurarah, who said: I heard Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} say: 
"The one making a will has the right to revoke it, whether he is in good health or ill."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.12 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.199 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.189 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.73 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.303  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 93, H. 5458
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٣، ح ٥٤٥٨

hadiths.chapter: 93. CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
hadiths.ar:
5460  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ي ُّون ُّسَّ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ِا ِسْن َّاذِةِ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم   « :
مِن ْ وَّصِي  َّي ِهِ ق َّي ُّعْي ِق َّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ إ َّمَّزَّ ن ِي َّمْلِي كِهِ وَّ ي ُّمَّلِّك َّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ إ َّمَّزَّ ن ِعِي ْق ِ هِ وَّ ي ُّعْطِى َّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ جَّزَّمَّهُّ وَّ ي َّحْزِمَّ مَّن ْ كَّان َّ 
إ َّعْطَّاةُّ مَّا لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ رَّج َّغَّ عَّن ْه
hadiths.en:
Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman narrated from Abdullah ibn Muskan from Abu 
Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
"The Commander of the Faithful {a.s} ruled that the manumitted slave (Al-mudabbar) is 
to be freed from one -third of the estate. A man has the right to revoke his will, to add 
to it or reduce from it, as long as he has not passed away."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.12 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.199 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.190 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.74 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.302  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 93, H. 5459
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٣، ح ٥٤٥٩

hadiths.chapter: 93. CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman with his chain of transmission, 
Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain {a.s} said: 
"A man has the right to alter his will. He may free the one he had initially ordered to be 
owned, and he may grant ownership to the one he had initially ordered to be freed. He 
may give to the one he had previously deprived and deprive the one he had previ ously 
granted, as long as he has not retracted from his decision."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.199

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 93, H. 5460
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON REVOKING A BEQUEST
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٩٣، ح ٥٤٦٠

hadiths.chapter: 94. CHAPTER ON ONE WHO MAKES A BEQUEST
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} regarding a man who made a will in the presence of his heirs, and they 
approved it. However, after the man's death, they attempted to revoke the will.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They do not have the right to revoke it. The will remains binding upon 
them once they had approved it during his lifetime." 
[AL SADUQ] 
Similarly, Ṣafwan ibn Ya hya narrated from Man ṣur ibn Hazim from Abu Abdullah {a.s} 
with the same ruling. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.12 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.200 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.193 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.193 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.122 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.122 • Nuzhat Al-Nazir, Vol.1 
p.92 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.275 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.51 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.283

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 94, H. 5461

reference.location: Volume 4 › CHAPTER ON ONE WHO MAKES A BEQUEST

hadiths.chapter: 95. CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION OF EXECUTING THE
hadiths.ar:
5463  -  وَّ رَّوَّى سَّهْلُّ ن ْن ُّ ر ِي َّاذٍ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إَّلْوَّلِي ذِ عَّن ْ ي ُّون ُّسَّ ن ْن ِ ي َّعْق ُّوب َّ : إ َّن  َّ رَّج ُّلاً كَّان َّ ن ِهَّمَّذ َّإن َّ ذ كَّزَّ إ َّن  َّ إ َّن َّاةُّ مَّاب َّ  
وَّ كَّان َّ لأَّ ي َّعْزِف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلْأ َّمْزَّ ق َّا َّوْصَّى ن ِوَّصِي  َّه ٍ عِن ْذَّ إَّلْمَّوْب ِ وَّ إ َّوْصَّى إ َّن ْ ي ُّعْطَّى  س َّى ْء  ق ِى  سَّن ِي لِ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّسُّن ِلَّ عَّن ْهُّ إ َّن ُّو 
عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ كَّي ْف َّ ي ُّق ْعَّلُّ ن ِهِ وَّ إ َّج ْن َّزْن َّاةُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ كَّان َّ لأَّ ي َّعْزِف ُّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلْأ َّمْزَّ وَّ إ َّوْ صَّى ن ِوَّصِي  َّه ٍ عِن ْذَّ إَّلْمَّوْب ِ ق َّق َّال 
إ َّن  َّ رَّج ُّلاً إ َّوْصَّى إ ِلَّى  َّ  إ َّن ْ إ َّص َّغَّ مَّالَّهُّ ق ِى  ي َّهُّوذِى ٍّ إ َّوْ ن َّصْزَّإن ِى ٍّ لَّوَّص َّعْي ُّهُّ ق ِي هِمْ إ ِن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ي َّق ُّولُّ  ۝  ق َّمَّن ْ ن َّذ َّلَّهُّ 
ن َّعْذَّ مٰا سَّمِعَّهُّ ق َّا ِن  َّمٰا إ ِي ْمُّهُّ عَّلَّى إَّل َّذ ِي ن َّ ي ُّن َّذِّلُّون َّهُّ إ ِن  َّ إَّللّٰهَّ سَّمِي غ  عَّلِي م   ۝  ق َّان ْط ُّزْ إ ِلَّى مَّ ن ْ ي َّح ْزُّح ُّ ق ِى  هَّذ ِةِ إَّلْوُّج ُّوةِ ي َّعْن ِى  
إَّلي  ُّع ُّورَّ ق َّان ْعَّي ُّوإ ن ِهِ إ ِلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn ʿIsa narrated from Hariz from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said: 
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who made a will to give his wealth in the way of 
Allah {SWT}. 
Imam {a.s} replied: "Give it to the one he specified in his will, even if that person is a Jew 
or a Christian. Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic, says:  
'But whoever changes it after hearing it, the sin is only upon those who change it .'"  
(Surah Al-Baqarah 2:181) 
[AL SADUQ] 
The author of this book (may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him) commented:  
"What he has the right to bequeath is one-third (of his wealth)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.14 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.200 • Nuzhat Al-Nazir, Vol.1 p.11 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.203 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.129 • Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.85 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.19 
p.345 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.1 p.381 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.85 p.316 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Vol.1 p.159  
﴿HADITH ﴾
